meaning: vt.转移,传下,[生物] 退化,衰落vi.被移交
examples: The best companies are those that devolve responsibility as far as they can.
They devolve too little power to local staff and rarely promote non-Japanese to top management.
The Northern Ireland Assembly voted for a plan to devolve policing and justice powers to Belfast.
Plaid heritage spokeswoman Bethan Jenkins said it underlined the need to devolve responsibility for broadcasting.
The Welsh government wants the UK government to devolve powers over large-scale energy generation projects.
